stimulus.content:
A table shows data for two countries:

• Country X: Birth rate = 40 per 1,000 people, Death rate = 10 per 1,000
• Country Y: Birth rate = 10 per 1,000 people, Death rate = 9 per 1,000

stem: Based on the data, which country's population is growing faster, and why?

choices.text: Country X, because more people are being born than dying
Country Y, because it has a lower death rate overall
Country X, because it has the fewest total people
Country Y, because its birth rate is very low

answer_explanation: Country X has a much larger gap between births (40) and deaths (10), meaning 30 more people per 1,000 are added each year. Country Y only adds 1 person per 1,000, so it grows much slower.
